articles.title: Storms, Hail Lash Telangana After Heatwave - Deccan Chronicle
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are forecast till May 10, with maximum temperatures expected to fall by 2–3°C over the next three days.
Hyderabad:Rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds are likely to continue across Telangana for the next five days, bringing relief after a prolonged spell of 42°C‑plus temperatures, the IMD said. Yellow alerts have been issued for multiple districts, warning of thunderstorms with lightning and winds of 30–50 kmph. Hailstorms are also likely at isolated places in Adilabad, Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Nirmal, Khammam, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahbubabad and Nagarkurnool.
An IMD scientist said a north–south trough from north interior Karnataka to the Gulf of Mannar and an upper air cyclonic circulation over north Telangana are driving the current weather. Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are forecast till May 10, with maximum temperatures expected to fall by 2–3°C over the next three days.
Hyderabad recorded a drop in temperatures on Wednesday after crossing 42°C earlier this week. The city’s average maximum stood at 38°C, with Himayatnagar touching 40.5°C, Mehdipatnam 40.2°C and Secunderabad 40.1°C.
Across Telangana, Mosra in Nizamabad district recorded the highest at 42.8°C, followed by Adilabad at 42.6°C, while Bhadradri Kothagudem, Jagtial and Khammam crossed 42°C.
The IMD said Hyderabad is likely to witness partly cloudy skies with light to moderate rain or thundershowers during evening or night hours over the next 48 hours. Maximum and minimum temperatures are expected to hover around 37°C and 26°C.
